description:
Configure Kubernetes RBAC to grant the `pipeline-operator` permission to update the `status` subresource of `datapipelines.etl.data.com`, and use RBAC Aggregation to automatically extend the cluster's default `view` and `edit` roles for developer access. 

A custom CRD `datapipelines.etl.data.com` is installed, but the operator deployment in the `data-system` namespace is failing to update the `status` subresource due to missing permissions in the `pipeline-operator-role` ClusterRole. Additionally, developers provisioned via the default `view` and `edit` roles currently receive `Forbidden` errors when attempting to read or create `datapipelines`.

Update the `pipeline-operator-role` ClusterRole to grant `update` and `patch` verbs specifically on the `datapipelines/status` resource. Create and label new ClusterRoles (e.g., `datapipeline-viewer` and `datapipeline-editor`) to aggregate permissions into the default `view` and `edit` roles. The viewer role must grant `get`, `list`, and `watch` verbs, while the editor role must grant `create`, `update`, `patch`, `delete`, and `deletecollection` verbs. Ensure the principle of least privilege is maintained: standard developers using the `edit` role must not be able to modify the `status` subresource.

Login to Rancher at https://localhost with credentials admin / Admin12345678!
